title: Neanotis foetida
intro: Neanotis foetida, also known as the foul-smelling Neanotis, is a flowering plant species in the family Gentianaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neanotis foetida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Neanotis foetida is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, often combined with other herbs, and is used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Neanotis foetida belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the madder family. It is a species of flowering plant that is native to parts of Asia. Neanotis foetida is further classified into the genus Neanotis, which is a group of flowering plants closely related to the genus Anotis. The classification of Neanotis foetida is also recognized at the species level, where it is a distinct entity within its genus. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and genetic characteristics.
uses: Neanotis foetida is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions. Its roots are used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues.
